kill_fact: No kill_fact breaks the (bearish) PASS thesis — every load-bearing number verifies against primary sources and the verification REINFORCES the bear: FY2025 net sales $21.75B (-3% YoY) with GAAP consolidated operating margin 10.0%, FY2025 adjusted EPS only $2.59, and Q1'26 adjusted EPS $0.57 DOWN 12% YoY (GAAP $0.28, organic sales -1%) on net debt of $10.787B. The +500% data-center order print is real but DC is a $1B->$1.5B line (~2.3%) on a $22B base; the consolidated P&L is flat-to-shrinking, margin-compressing and sub-WACC, and the ~24x forward multiple already re-rated on the fired/priced AI-cooling catalyst. The bear kill (no-growth guide at a growth multiple, no resolution-driver independence) survives intact — this is a legitimate verdict-C PASS.
corrections: Minor figure precision only: (1) net debt is $10.278B at FY2025 year-end and $10.787B at 3/31/2026 per the 8-Ks, not '~$10.5B' — the debate's rounding straddles the two but should cite the dated value. (2) The debate implies a 2026E base ~$2.80 off a higher prior; actual FY2025 adjusted EPS was $2.59, so the '~$2.80 guide' is ~+8% YoY adjusted-EPS growth (buyback-aided), worth stating explicitly. (3) The $1.5B DC target / 'targeting to exceed' language is sourced to the Q4-2025 release & call, NOT the Q1'26 8-K (which only says backlog 'fully covers expected 2026 data center sales') — attribute it correctly. None of these alter the verdict or risk/reward.
evidence: Carrier Global FY2025 8-K / IR release (filed Feb 2026): FY2025 net sales $21.75B (-3% YoY, organic -1%), adjusted EPS $2.59, GAAP operating margin 10.0% (adjusted 15.1%), net debt $10.278B (total debt $11.833B); 2026 outlook ~$22B sales, ~$2.80 adj EPS, flat-to-LSD organic, ~$2B FCF; $1.5B 2026 DC sales target with ~$1B backlog, 'targeting to exceed' — ir.carrier.com, 2026-02

Carrier Global Q1 2026 8-K (SEC EDGAR a99-q12026earningsexhibit, filed Apr 2026): revenue $5.34B (+2% reported, -1% organic), adjusted EPS $0.57 (-12% YoY), GAAP EPS $0.28, total orders +11%, Commercial HVAC orders +35% incl. data-center orders +500%, FCF -$15M, net debt $10.787B at 3/31/2026, FY2026 ~$22B / ~$2.80 adj EPS reaffirmed (not raised) — sec.gov, 2026-04

Carrier Q4 2025 earnings call (Gitlin): Q4 North American / CSA data-center orders up ~400-500%, ~$1B 2026 backlog, committed to $1.5B 2026 DC revenue 'targeting to exceed' — finance.yahoo.com / 247wallst.com, 2026-03
